author: mark
text:
Suppress legacy-plugin clap 'unrecognized subcommand' on describe

Runner::describe() probes a plugin for SPEC §5.2 self-description; a clap-built legacy plugin replies with 'error: unrecognized subcommand' and exits non-zero. The fall-through to the legacy shim is intentional, but parse_outcome's 'describe failed' warning was leaking the misleading clap error on every bl invocation against balls-plugin-github(-issues).

Detect the clap signature in stderr and return Ok(None) silently. Real wire failures (timeout, malformed JSON, other non-success exits) still warn. Legacy test fixture now emits clap's actual stderr, exercised by a new tests/plugin_describe_silent.rs regression.
